AAPL.reasoning: Three of five strategy agents output neutral on AAPL; two output a low-conviction bullish lean (both capped at confidence 42). The consensus is neutral, reinforced by the macro agent's explicit R/R veto argument. The market context here is transitional-to-cautious: Fed higher-for-longer, Dow on five consecutive losing sessions, Meta -9% capex shock creating risk-off in mega-cap tech — this environment weights swing_macro_context heavily per strategy weighting rules. The macro agent calculates immediate R/R to first resistance of only 0.71:1 and extended R/R of 1.92:1 (below the 2:1 minimum), which is a structural reason to pass independent of sentiment. The two bullish signals are internally hedged: swing_mean_reversion notes the ideal entry window has 'partially passed' and the current 23.6% Fib retracement is a shallow pullback needing a deeper test at 38.2% ($264.21) to be clean; swing_catalyst_news is explicitly a binary pre-earnings trade with a 1-3 day horizon that expires at open tomorrow. Critically, tomorrow's Q2 earnings make this a binary event — not a swing trade setup. Earnings-day holds require a fundamentally different risk framework than swing trading. ADX at 15.78 (trend_momentum's disqualifier) and volume at 0.73x average (breakout's disqualifier) both confirm that price is in a low-conviction consolidation zone rather than a directional setup. Recommended action: hold existing positions if any; do not initiate new swing positions ahead of the binary catalyst. Reassess post-earnings once the binary resolves and a new swing structure forms.
AAPL.key_conflicts: The core conflict is between swing_catalyst_news and swing_macro_context. Catalyst_news argues analyst consensus (31 Buy, avg target $299-305), strong Q2 guidance (13-16% revenue growth YoY), and India production ramp support a pre-earnings drift trade — bullish at 42 confidence. Macro_context counters that R/R fails the 2:1 minimum at current price, the macro regime is risk-off (Iran energy inflation, Meta contagion, Dow losing streak), and no sector inflow signal confirms the thesis — neutral at 55 confidence. The resolution: catalyst_news is specifically a 1-3 day pre-earnings hold expiring tomorrow at open, which is not a swing trade by definition; macro_context's R/R analysis applies to the swing horizon and holds veto weight in a transitional regime. A secondary conflict exists between swing_mean_reversion (bullish, noting Fib 23.6% bounce in progress) and swing_trend_momentum (neutral, flagging that the bounce entry has 'partially passed' and ADX remains disqualifying). Mean_reversion's own reasoning acknowledges the cleaner setup would be a deeper 38.2% Fib retest at $264.21 — meaning both agents effectively agree: wait for a better entry. The insider selling signal (-$235M net, CEO Tim Cook distributed $12M+ at $251-256 in April) cuts against the bullish lean from two agents and is unresolved by any bullish argument. Breakout agent is aligned with neutral — no confirmed close above $276.11 resistance, volume too thin to validate. No high-conviction setup exists today.
